{"tool": "python_exec", "arguments": {
"code": "from collections import deque, defaultdict;\
nats = deque(["australian", "french", "japanese", "mexican", "pakistani"]);\
hobbies = deque(["chess", "dancing", "gardening", "singing", "video-games"]);\
foods = deque(["avocado", "corn", "kiwi", "papaya", "plum"]);\
pets = deque(["cat", "frog", "lizard", "rabbit", "snake"]);\
\
nats.pop(); # remove pakistani from the list to check positions\
pos_chess = next((i for i, hobby in enumerate(hobbies) if hobby == 'chess'))\
\
nats.append(pakistani); # put pakistani back\
\
if pos_chess % 2 == 0: # chess is on an even position\
  ans = nats[pos_chess // 2]\
else:\
  ans = nats[pos_chess // 2 + 1]\
\
print(ans)"
}}

This tool call uses Python to find the nationality of the person who has chess by checking the parity position of chess in the line and then finding the nationality at that position.
